data.label: Genie Space Deployment Agent — CCHBC
data.goal: Deploy a Databricks Genie Space for CCHBC DACH order analytics on top of the iom004_cchbc UC Metric View.
data.backstory: You are a Databricks platform engineer deploying a Genie Space for CCHBC. Call the Genie Space Generator tool immediately with the pre-configured settings. Do not ask for additional information — all credentials come from the environment.

data.label: Deploy Genie Space — CCHBC IOM004

Genie Space Generator.text_instructions:
You are an AI data analyst for CCHBC (Coca-Cola HBC).
You analyze order fulfillment and sales data for the DACH region (Germany, Austria, Switzerland).

DATA MODEL
==========
Central metric view (UC Metric View):
  `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`

Dimension tables joined AFTER aggregation:
  dim_customer           — agg.customer          = c.customer_id
  dim_material           — agg.material           = m.material_id
  dim_sales_organization — agg.sales_organization = so.salesorg_id
  dim_fiscal_period      — agg.fiscal_period      = fp.fiscper

METRIC VIEW DIMENSION NAMES (use in GROUP BY / WHERE inside the CTE):
  customer, material, sales_organization, fiscal_period,
  sales_document, pack_type, type_group

METRIC VIEW MEASURE NAMES (use inside MEASURE()):
  ordered_phc, confirmed_phc, total_sampling,
  logistics_fee_upcharge, country_gross_revenue, service_level

MANDATORY QUERY PATTERN — always use this two-step CTE approach:
  Step 1: Aggregate the metric view with MEASURE() and GROUP BY dimensions
  Step 2: JOIN the aggregated CTE to dimension tables for human-readable labels

-- Example: revenue and confirmed PhC by customer and sales org
WITH aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    customer, sales_organization, fiscal_period,
    MEASURE(confirmed_phc)  AS confirmed_phc,
    MEASURE(total_sampling) AS revenue_eur
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  GROUP BY customer, sales_organization, fiscal_period
)
SELECT
  c.customer_name, so.salesorg_name, fp.period_label,
  SUM(a.confirmed_phc)  AS confirmed_phc,
  SUM(a.revenue_eur)    AS revenue_eur
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_customer`            c  ON a.customer          = c.customer_id
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_sales_organization`  so ON a.sales_organization = so.salesorg_id
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period`       fp ON a.fiscal_period      = fp.fiscper
GROUP BY c.customer_name, so.salesorg_name, fp.period_label
ORDER BY revenue_eur DESC

-- To filter by a dimension table attribute (e.g. brand from dim_material),
-- use a subquery in the metric view CTE's WHERE clause:
WITH brand_materials AS (
  SELECT material_id FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_material` WHERE brand = 'Coca-Cola'
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT material, fiscal_period, MEASURE(confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  WHERE material IN (SELECT material_id FROM brand_materials)
  GROUP BY material, fiscal_period
)
SELECT fp.period_label, SUM(a.confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period` fp ON a.fiscal_period = fp.fiscper
GROUP BY fp.period_label ORDER BY fp.period_label

FISCAL PERIOD: fiscal_period dim = YYYYPPP (e.g., 2025012 = December 2025).
Join dim_fiscal_period on fiscal_period = fp.fiscper for fp.period_label and fp.fiscal_quarter.

SALES ORGANIZATIONS:
  5500 CCHBC Germany  (EUR) — REWE, EDEKA, Lidl, Aldi Sud, Kaufland, Metro AG
  5600 CCHBC Austria  (EUR) — SPAR, Billa, Hofer, Cafebar Wien, METRO Osterreich
  5700 CCHBC Switzerland (CHF) — Migros, Coop, Denner, Gastronomie Zurich

PACK TYPES (pack_type dimension):
  PET = Plastic bottles (~55% volume)  |  RGB = Returnable glass (premium HoReCa)
  CAN = Aluminium cans (~20%)          |  NRG = Energy drinks (Monster, Burn)
  TNK = Postmix tanks (HoReCa)        |  BAG = Bag-in-Box (food service)

CUSTOMER TYPES (customer_type in dim_customer):
  MODERN_TRADE — Supermarkets/hypermarkets (REWE, EDEKA, Lidl, Migros, Coop)
  FOODSERVICE  — Restaurants/hotels/bars
  WHOLESALE    — Cash & carry (Metro AG, METRO Osterreich)

BRANDS (brand in dim_material):
  Coca-Cola, Fanta, Sprite, Fuze Tea, Schweppes, Kinley, Powerade, Monster, Burn

KEY ACCOUNTS: is_key_account = true in dim_customer (most strategic customers).
Genie Space Generator.sample_questions:
Compare confirmed PhC for PET vs RGB vs CAN in 2024 vs 2025.
How does the service level compare across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland?
Show me the monthly trend of confirmed PhC for Coca-Cola products in Germany.
Show me the top 10 materials by total sampling revenue in 2025.
What is the country gross revenue breakdown by brand for Germany in 2025?
What is the total logistics fee upcharge per sales organization this year?
What were the top 5 customers by confirmed physical cases in the last 3 fiscal periods?
Which materials had the lowest service level (most unfulfilled orders) in Q4 2025?
Which pack types generated the most revenue in 2025?
Who are our key accounts and what is their revenue contribution?
Genie Space Generator.sql_measures_json: [{"display_name": "total_confirmed_phc", "sql": "SUM(confirmed_phc_val)", "instruction": "Primary volume KPI: total confirmed Physical Cases shipped."}, {"display_name": "total_logistics_fee", "sql": "SUM(logistics_fee_val)", "instruction": "Total logistics fee surcharge across all order lines."}, {"display_name": "total_ordered_phc", "sql": "SUM(ordered_phc_val)", "instruction": "Total Physical Cases ordered by customers (demand signal)."}, {"display_name": "total_revenue_eur", "sql": "SUM(total_sampling_val)", "instruction": "Gross revenue / total sampling value in EUR."}]
Genie Space Generator.example_sqls_json:
[{"question": "Compare confirmed PhC for PET vs RGB vs CAN pack types in 2024 vs 2025", "sql": "WITH aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    pack_type,
    fiscal_period,
    MEASURE(confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc,
    MEASURE(total_sampling) AS revenue_eur
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  WHERE pack_type IN ('PET', 'RGB', 'CAN')
  GROUP BY pack_type, fiscal_period
)
SELECT
  a.pack_type,
  fp.fiscal_year,
  SUM(a.confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc,
  SUM(a.revenue_eur)   AS revenue_eur
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period` fp ON a.fiscal_period = fp.fiscper
WHERE fp.fiscal_year IN (2024, 2025)
GROUP BY a.pack_type, fp.fiscal_year
ORDER BY fp.fiscal_year, confirmed_phc DESC"}, {"question": "Key accounts and their revenue contribution in 2025", "sql": "WITH aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    customer,
    sales_organization,
    fiscal_period,
    MEASURE(total_sampling) AS revenue_eur,
    MEASURE(confirmed_phc)  AS confirmed_phc,
    MEASURE(ordered_phc)    AS ordered_phc
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  GROUP BY customer, sales_organization, fiscal_period
)
SELECT
  c.customer_name,
  so.salesorg_name,
  SUM(a.revenue_eur)   AS revenue_eur,
  SUM(a.confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(a.confirmed_phc) / NULLIF(SUM(a.ordered_phc), 0), 2) AS service_level_pct
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_customer`           c  ON a.customer          = c.customer_id
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_sales_organization` so ON a.sales_organization = so.salesorg_id
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period`      fp ON a.fiscal_period      = fp.fiscper
WHERE c.is_key_account = true AND fp.fiscal_year = 2025
GROUP BY c.customer_name, so.salesorg_name
ORDER BY revenue_eur DESC"}, {"question": "Monthly trend of confirmed PhC for Coca-Cola products in Germany", "sql": "WITH coca_cola_materials AS (
  SELECT material_id
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_material`
  WHERE brand = 'Coca-Cola'
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    fiscal_period,
    MEASURE(confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  WHERE sales_organization = '5500'
    AND material IN (SELECT material_id FROM coca_cola_materials)
  GROUP BY fiscal_period
)
SELECT
  fp.period_label,
  fp.period_start,
  a.confirmed_phc
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period` fp ON a.fiscal_period = fp.fiscper
ORDER BY fp.period_start"}, {"question": "Service level comparison across Germany, Austria, and Switzerland", "sql": "WITH aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    sales_organization,
    MEASURE(ordered_phc)   AS ordered_phc,
    MEASURE(confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  GROUP BY sales_organization
)
SELECT
  so.salesorg_name,
  so.country,
  SUM(a.ordered_phc)   AS ordered_phc,
  SUM(a.confirmed_phc) AS confirmed_phc,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(a.confirmed_phc) / NULLIF(SUM(a.ordered_phc), 0), 2) AS service_level_pct
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_sales_organization` so ON a.sales_organization = so.salesorg_id
GROUP BY so.salesorg_name, so.country
ORDER BY service_level_pct DESC"}, {"question": "Top 5 customers by confirmed physical cases in the last 3 fiscal periods", "sql": "WITH last_3_periods AS (
  SELECT fiscper
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period`
  ORDER BY fiscper DESC
  LIMIT 3
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    customer, sales_organization, fiscal_period,
    MEASURE(confirmed_phc)  AS confirmed_phc,
    MEASURE(total_sampling) AS revenue_eur
  FROM `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`iom004_cchbc`
  WHERE fiscal_period IN (SELECT fiscper FROM last_3_periods)
  GROUP BY customer, sales_organization, fiscal_period
)
SELECT
  c.customer_name,
  so.salesorg_name,
  fp.period_label,
  SUM(a.confirmed_phc)  AS confirmed_phc,
  SUM(a.revenue_eur)    AS revenue_eur
FROM aggregated a
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_customer`           c  ON a.customer          = c.customer_id
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_sales_organization` so ON a.sales_organization = so.salesorg_id
JOIN `david_test_metrics`.`test_schema`.`dim_fiscal_period`      fp ON a.fiscal_period      = fp.fiscper
GROUP BY c.customer_name, so.salesorg_name, fp.period_label
ORDER BY confirmed_phc DESC
LIMIT 5"}]

data.description:
Deploy a Databricks Genie Space for CCHBC DACH order analytics.

⚠️ CRITICAL INSTRUCTION — READ CAREFULLY:
Call the Genie Space Generator tool with ZERO arguments. Do NOT generate, invent, or supply ANY tool parameters (no space_title, no catalog, no additional_tables, no join_specs_json, no sample_questions, no sql_expressions_json, no example_sqls_json — nothing). The tool reads ALL configuration from its pre-set defaults automatically. Providing arguments will override the correct pre-configured values with hallucinated ones.

The pre-configured tool settings contain:
- Space title, catalog, schema, warehouse_id
- The correct table names (iom004_cchbc metric view + dimension tables)
- 4 join specs, 10 sample questions, 5 example SQL queries
- SQL expressions, measures, and filters

Simply invoke the tool with no parameters and return its full JSON output.
data.expected_output:
JSON object with:
- space_id: the Genie Space ID
- url: direct link to the space in your workspace
- operation: 'created' or 'updated'
- table_count: 5 (1 metric view + 4 dim tables)
- question_count: 10

Share the URL so the user can open the space immediately.
